author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Shipped the v2.12 actor-override-chain. `resolve_current_user`
now takes an `Option<&str>` for the `--actor` flag and walks
`actor_override > JJF_ACTOR env > jj config user.name >
NoCurrentUser`. `resolve_author` mirrors it with
`--author > JJF_ACTOR (synthesizes "$JJF_ACTOR <user.email>") >
jj config user.name + user.email > MissingAuthor`. A new
`jjf_actor_env()` helper centralizes the empty-string
fall-through rule (empty / whitespace skips the slot).

Per the orchestrator's Choice A: `--actor` lives on
`Commands::Update` only. `Commands::Comment` keeps its existing
`--author` flag (free-form `Name <email>`); the env var gives
both verbs a unified surface. `jjf ready --claim` picks up the
env-var slot automatically through the shared resolver.

`--actor` declares `conflicts_with_all = [unclaim, assignee,
unset_assignee]` so clap rejects the ambiguous combinations.
The `NoCurrentUser` error message now mentions `JJF_ACTOR`.

Files changed: `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` (resolver chain,
`Commands::Update.actor` flag + plumbing, error-hint copy),
`crates/jjf/tests/actor.rs` (new — 12 integration tests),
`docs/cli-json.md` (v2.11 → v2.12 changelog entry),
`CLAUDE.md` (one-line in the Updating-issues block plus a
multi-agent-attribution paragraph under "Dispatch serially").

# Recommendation

Close. All acceptance bullets met: flag, env, config fallback,
empty-string fall-through (both flag and env), clap conflicts,
comment author chain, unit + integration tests, docs and
CLAUDE.md updated.

Workspace tests: 615 pass (was 602, +13). Smoke verified end-
to-end with the release binary on a throwaway issue
(`JJF_ACTOR=alice ... --claim` → assignee alice; `--actor bob`
overrides env to bob; `--actor "" + JJF_ACTOR=fallback-env`
falls through to fallback-env).

# Confidence

High. The chain semantics match the spec exactly; the
fall-through-on-empty rule is exercised by integration tests in
both directions (flag-empty falls through to env;
env-empty falls through to config). Env-var hygiene in tests is
fork-only (`Command::env`); no in-process `set_var`.

# Open follow-ups

None blocking. Worth noting: `jj_config_get("user.name")`
reads ALL jj config scopes (repo, user, global). The "no
current user" integration test had to point `JJ_CONFIG` at a
nonexistent path to make the global Tester fallback disappear
— a real fan-out won't hit this (orchestrators that want
distinct names will set `JJF_ACTOR`). The `--actor`/env
chain stops at jj config; we deliberately did NOT add a
`$USER` fallback (per the orchestrator's scope note).
